chief_complaint: A 79-year-old man presented with a history of acute myocardial infarction.
history: The patient underwent percutaneous coronary intervention (PCI) for acute myocardial infarction (AMI). Subsequent transthoracic echocardiogram (TTE) revealed an apical pseudoaneurysm. Follow-up TTE and cardiac magnetic resonance (CMR) confirmed the pseudoaneurysm involving both ventricles with secondary left-to-right shunt.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings were not provided.
labs: ECG: Acute myocardial infarction. Bloods: Not provided. Imaging: TTE and CMR confirmed the pseudoaneurysm involving both ventricles with secondary left-to-right shunt.
correct_diagnosis: Apical left ventricular pseudoaneurysm with left-to-right shunt
differentials: Apical left ventricular pseudoaneurysm with left-to-right shunt
correct_ranking: Apical left ventricular pseudoaneurysm with left-to-right shunt
